Alright, custom fitness, let's dig into it. So we are going to be starting off with a blueprint and the blueprint gives examples of calorie burning activities. Right? So we're listing different examples of things they can do to burn calories, not just specifically what you tell them to do. It also encourages them to get active and to do things outside of the gym that burns calories, which is really good for trying to transform into a healthy lifestyle. Okay, second, it allows you to walk them through the realization that they need to exercise. Okay, basically if they cannot burn enough calories, then they need to change their goals, which nobody wants to do. They'll never do that. They'll be like, fine, I'll find a way to burn more. So as an example, if you say like, look, we're gonna get the majority of our results from nutrition, but fitness is gonna be extremely important. So we need to burn 3,500 calories per week to hit your goals. How are we going to do it? And they can only think of three workouts a week that burn 500 calories a piece at 1500. You'd be like, that's not, that's not gonna do it. So we need to go back and adjust your goals. And they'll be like, no, no, no, no, I think I can squeeze in a workout here, here, and here. Okay, that's more like it. Let's do that instead. And they'll be like, awesome. Right. So it allows you to get them to, nobody wants to change their goals. Nobody wants to get worse results. So it allows you to leverage that to get them to exercise more. Okay, now the member portal. Right. So it comes with pre-built workouts in there already, but it allows you all the framework. And it also yields one pound of fat if they're burning an additional 300, or I'm sorry, 300 or 3,500 calories in addition to what they normally burn, just being alive and doing stuff. Right. So here are the examples you can add to them if you'd like, but light cardio, moderate cardio. So you can, you can put these below, you can drag and drop these below by writing them in and then just multiply it by how many minutes they think that they can do it for. So if somebody is already doing like an hour jog in the morning, I like typically wouldn't stop them from doing that. I'll just write it in there. So I can write in Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays, 600 calories. Why? Because 30 minutes times two, would be 60 minutes. So they're our jog. And then so 300 calories times two would be 600. And I can write there on Monday, Wednesday, Friday, 600 calories jog. Right. And then you can put your workouts in there that they're going to be doing either at your gym or online or wherever. You can write those in there. But no matter what, when you add up Monday through Sunday, you want to hit that 3,500 calories. And if you can't, you're going to have to talk with them about where you can squeeze some more calories in there. I could totally do this and be like, awesome. You should do that. So that's how this portion works. And you just put the totals in underneath. And that's your way of customizing it based on specific areas that they want to focus on. Or they say, I really want to tone my butt, you know, so then you are going to have them do a lot of squats and variations of squats and things like that. And you can send them to a specific part of your training. So it's still totally scalable. You just have to know which videos those are. And you can write that on your blueprint and say, I want you to do these this many days a week. And that's going to help you focus on the area that you want to focus on. Then ideal days off, you're going to write in there just so you guys are on the same page as far as what days they're not working out, but more so so people don't work out seven days a week without ever taking a break. So you want to use this time to explain to them that they should be taking days off at least one or two days a week and not training seven days a week nonstop ever. Because chances are they're not, they don't have the correct amount of supplementation and nutrition to recover like that. It's just, it's not really healthy. And you guys know that use your own judgment. So underneath that, it's going to say, Hey, we're going to send you the link for the, our own proprietary at home workouts. Right? So you'll need to maybe change the workouts that you're having them do. If they're, if they're really finding it that easy, and it's not a bad thing.
